IdempotencyKey.description: OPTIONAL but STRONGLY RECOMMENDED on every mutating call. The first request with a given key under the calling tenant runs normally; replays of the same (route, key) within the TTL (default 24h) return the cached response verbatim with the `Idempotent-Replay: true` response header. Replays against a different route for the same key return `409 idempotency_route_mismatch`. Suggested format: UUIDv4 (Stripe-style semantics; slice 11a).
IdempotencyKey.name: Idempotency-Key

code.description: Stable machine-readable error code. See SPEC §9.
code.title: Code

effective_truncation_cap_bytes.description: Per-stream byte cap used for stdout/stderr truncation.
effective_truncation_cap_bytes.title: Effective Truncation Cap Bytes

note.description: Operator-friendly note attached to the token.
note.title: Note

scopes.description: Optional list of scopes this token may use. None / omitted = grant all scopes (back-compat default). Empty list = explicitly no scopes (token can only call routes that require no scope, useful for read-only health probes).
scopes.title: Scopes

token.description: Plaintext bearer token. Saved nowhere by the service; the operator must record it now.
token.title: Token

workspace_mib.description: Per-session hard cap on /workspace usage, in MiB. Enforced by XFS prjquota when SANDBOX_VOLUME_BASE is on an XFS or ext4+prjquota filesystem; advisory on network storage. Pair with the coarser tenant-level TenantLimits.max_workspace_gib (in GiB) — the two units are deliberate.
workspace_mib.title: Workspace Mib

argv.description: WARNING: argv is visible to any token holding the `processes` scope (via process_list / process_get). Pass credentials via `env`, never as positional args. Operators handling sensitive workloads should issue tokens without `processes` scope.
argv.title: Argv

old_token_grace_seconds.description: How long the previous token continues to authenticate.
old_token_grace_seconds.title: Old Token Grace Seconds

token.description: The new bearer token in plaintext. Save it now; the API never returns it again. The previous token continues to authenticate for `old_token_grace_seconds` so callers have time to switch.
token.title: Token

name.description: Operator-friendly label for the process. Free-form.
name.title: Name

TenantLimits.description:
Per-tenant overrides for the global Settings.tenant_max_*
defaults. Each field None ⇒ inherit the global default.

max_workspace_gib.description: Tenant-level coarse cap on workspace usage, in GiB. Used for policy / governance at the tenant tier. Per-session caps live on Limits.workspace_mib (in MiB) for finer granularity — the two units are deliberate.
max_workspace_gib.title: Max Workspace Gib

TenantLimits.title: TenantLimits

bearerAuth.description: Tenant-scoped opaque bearer token. Issued via `POST /v1/tenants/{tenant_id}/tokens` (admin) or bootstrapped from `SANDBOX_API_TOKEN`. Stored as HMAC-SHA256(pepper, plaintext) at rest (SPEC-405). Rotate via `POST /v1/tenants/me/tokens/rotate`; previous tokens authenticate during a 5-minute grace window.

info.description:
Control plane for an LLM-agent sandbox service. See
[SPECIFICATION.md](https://github.com/JISUlicious/sandboxing/blob/main/SPECIFICATION.md)
and
[ARCHITECTURE.md](https://github.com/JISUlicious/sandboxing/blob/main/ARCHITECTURE.md)
for the full contract.

Every session is a long-lived, hardened container. Filesystem state in
`/workspace` persists across exec calls and across stop/resume; process
state does not (SPEC-002). All endpoints under `/v1/sessions` require a
bearer token in the `Authorization` header.
info.title: Sandbox Service

get.description: Returns 200 as long as the process is up. Does not check Docker — see `/readyz`.
Prometheus text exposition (SPEC-503). No auth — bind to an internal port or restrict via reverse proxy in production.

Returns the health of subsystems the API needs to serve mutations: `docker` for the daemon, `audit` for the fail-closed audit log (ARCH §7). All-true means the service will accept new lifecycle / exec / file calls.

post.description: Creates a long-lived sandbox session under the calling tenant. Starts in `RUNNING` with an empty `/workspace` (SPEC-101). `limits` are merged with tenant-default values; per-field caps are enforced (SPEC-100, SPEC-300). Per-field violations return `400 limit_exceeded`; tenant concurrency caps return `429 limit_exceeded`.

Idempotent-Replay.description: Set to `true` when this response was replayed from the idempotency cache rather than freshly computed.

400.description: Validation failure: `invalid_argument`, `invalid_path`, or `limit_exceeded` (per-field request limits exceed tenant caps; SPEC-100).

429.description: Tenant concurrency or per-field limit exceeded (`limit_exceeded`). SPEC §6.

delete.description: Removes the container and its `/workspace` volume. Irreversible (SPEC-105). The destroy is multi-step (DESTROYING → DESTROYED) and idempotent on partial failure (ARCH-051).

Idempotent-Replay.description: Set to `true` when this response was replayed from the idempotency cache rather than freshly computed.

404.description: Session not found, not owned by the calling tenant, or already DESTROYED (SPEC-200).
Session not found, not owned by the calling tenant, or already DESTROYED (SPEC-200).

get.summary: Get a session's status and limits

post.description:
Runs `argv` inside the session and returns stdout/stderr/exit after the process exits or `timeout_s` is hit. Output is capped at 8 MiB per stream independently (SPEC-203); `truncated_streams` lists any that hit the cap. Stdin must be UTF-8 ≤ 1 MiB; binary stdin is a future enhancement.

STOPPED / IDLE sessions are transparently resumed before the exec runs (SPEC-104). `HTTP_PROXY`, `HTTPS_PROXY`, and `NO_PROXY` are forbidden in `env` (SPEC-201).

Idempotent-Replay.description: Set to `true` when this response was replayed from the idempotency cache rather than freshly computed.

400.description: Validation failure: `invalid_argument`, `invalid_path`, or `limit_exceeded` (per-field request limits exceed tenant caps; SPEC-100).

404.description: Session not found, not owned by the calling tenant, or already DESTROYED (SPEC-200).

408.description: Exec exceeded its wall-clock budget (`exec_timeout`). SPEC-201.

409.description: Session is in a state that doesn't allow this operation (`invalid_state`).

post.description:
Same contract as `/exec` but streams output live as SSE events.

**Event types** (each is an SSE frame with `event:` + `data:`):
- `event: stdout` / `event: stderr` — `{"chunk_b64": "<base64>"}`. Decoded chunks reconstruct the original byte stream in order. Once the per-stream 8 MiB cap is hit, no further chunks of that stream are emitted.
- `event: truncated` — `{"stream": "stdout"|"stderr"}`. Emitted exactly once per affected stream when the cap is reached.
- `event: result` — final ExecResponse-shaped payload (matches the synchronous `/exec` response, SPEC-201/202).
- `event: error` — `{"message": "..."}`. The producer thread crashed; the client should treat this as a 5xx.

Stdin is rejected with 400 — combining stdin with live demuxed streaming is a future slice. OpenAPI tooling does not generate useful clients for SSE; consume with a streaming HTTP client.

**Buffering caveat**: this endpoint streams chunks as the Docker daemon delivers them — there is no server-side accumulation. However, the program being executed may itself buffer its output. glibc's `stdio` block-buffers FD 1 with a 4–8 KiB buffer when stdout is a pipe (which is true under `docker exec`), so a C/Python program that writes small `printf` / `print(...)` bursts will appear to deliver its output all at once at process exit, despite this endpoint correctly streaming what the program emits.
To get incremental output regardless of buffer sizes:
- Bash builtin `echo` writes via `write(2)` directly —   no buffering. Streams natively.
- Stderr (FD 2) is line-buffered by default in glibc —   redirect with `>&2` for incremental progress messages.
- Wrap with `stdbuf -oL -eL <cmd>` to force line-buffering   on stdout/stderr.
- For Python: `python -u`, or `print(..., flush=True)`,   or `PYTHONUNBUFFERED=1` in `env`.
- For Node: `process.stdout.write(...)` is unbuffered;   `console.log` flushes on `\n` so it streams naturally.

get.description: Lists immediate children of `dir` (relative to `/workspace`; default is `/workspace` itself).

post.description: Writes a single file into `/workspace`. `content_b64` is base64-encoded for binary safety. Path is validated against traversal (`..`, absolute paths, NUL bytes) per SPEC-107. Parent directories are created as needed. STOPPED / IDLE sessions are transparently resumed (ARCH §3.3).

delete.description: Deletes a file under `/workspace`. Directories require `?recursive=true`; deleting `/workspace` itself is rejected (SPEC-107). Missing paths return 404.

parameters.description: Required to delete a non-empty directory.

get.description: Returns the file's raw bytes as `application/octet-stream`. The unix mode is exposed in the `X-File-Mode` response header. Reading a directory returns 400; missing path returns 404.

400.description: Validation failure: `invalid_argument`, `invalid_path`, or `limit_exceeded` (per-field request limits exceed tenant caps; SPEC-100).

post.description: Writes a single file into `/workspace`. Mirrors the `GET /files/{path}` and `DELETE /files/{path}` shapes: the path is in the URL and the body is the raw file content (`application/octet-stream`). Use this when the caller has bytes-in-hand — for base64-encoded JSON, see the collection-level `POST /files`. Path validation, parent-dir creation, and STOPPED-session resume are identical to the JSON variant.

requestBody.description: Raw file bytes. Empty body creates a zero-length file (touch-like).

get.description:
Server-Sent Events stream of the process's combined stdout+stderr log. Each SSE frame is `event: log` with a `data: {"chunk_b64": "<base64>"}` payload — base64 keeps binary log content (ANSI escapes, NULs) round-tripping cleanly. The stream stays open until the client disconnects or the underlying process exits.

OpenAPI tooling does not generate useful clients for SSE; consume with a streaming HTTP client (`httpx`, `curl -N`, etc.).

200.description: SSE stream of `log` events with base64-encoded chunks.

400.description: Validation failure: `invalid_argument`, `invalid_path`, or `limit_exceeded` (per-field request limits exceed tenant caps; SPEC-100).

get.summary: Tail a process's combined stdout+stderr log over SSE

post.description: Restarts the container for a `STOPPED` or `IDLE` session. Process state from before the stop is gone — only filesystem state persists (ARCH §3.4).

post.description: Stops the underlying container; the volume and `/workspace` contents are retained (SPEC-104). Resume implicitly via `/exec` or any file op, or explicitly via `/resume`.

post.description: Issues a new bearer token for the calling tenant and marks the current token revoked-at = now + grace seconds. Both tokens authenticate during the grace window so callers can read the response and switch over without downtime. After the window the old token returns 401. SPEC-405.

post.summary: Rotate the calling tenant's bearer token

delete.description: Revokes ALL tokens for the tenant immediately, marks all non-terminal sessions DESTROYING (the reaper will finish them on its next tick), and drops the tenants row. Irreversible.
